beats.reasoning: Short hop from REI; perfect weather and low energy suggest a gentle, scenic activity rather than pushing hard.

Nathan has a pattern of eating Molly Moon's salads (mentioned in recent days); returning to a familiar, quality spot fits his character and geography.

Early evening; time to head home for the wind-down before sleep, allowing a relaxed transition back to his neighborhood.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, review camping gear spreadsheet, and plan the week.
beats.reasoning: Nathan values quiet, purposeful time; preparing a meal (possibly salmon again, given his meal-prep habit) and organizing his meticulous expense spreadsheet for the upcoming camping trip fits his character perfectly.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: flip through fly-fishing catalogs, plan camping trip, and wind down.

Overnight sleep ending early Monday morning; Nathan gets a full 7+ hours to recover from low energy and prepare for the week ahead.

diary: Woke up early with that Sunday-morning feeling—coffee tasted better than usual. Drove out to Green Lake and just wandered the waterfront for a bit, watching the light hit the water. Stopped by REI, got lost in the fly-fishing section like I always do. Then hiked up Volunteer Park; the views were incredible today, city sprawled out below me, the water glittering. Grabbed a salad at Molly Moon's and people-watched for a while—there's something grounding about that. Spent the afternoon at Lake Union watching all the boats and paddleboards come and go, paddlers everywhere taking advantage of the weather. By the time I got home, I felt pleasantly tired. Spread out my camping gear spreadsheet, flipped through catalogs, started planning a trip. Just a quiet, perfect Sunday—the kind that reminds you why you stay in a city like this.
thought_process: Today was pure decompression. The weather was too good to waste, and I found myself gravitating toward water—Green Lake, then Lake Union. It's not random; I think being around water settles something in me, especially on days when work pressure eases. The REI stop was practical but also indulgent; I'm genuinely excited about a camping trip. Back home, I let myself linger with the catalogs and spreadsheets instead of rushing through planning. The mood never dipped today. I think the sunshine, the leisurely pace, and having space to daydream about trips ahead kept me in a genuinely good place. Sometimes the best days are the unstructured ones.
world_event_prompt: Calm, sunny Sunday, high of 72F. Quiet end-of-week day with no major disruptions. Lake Union sees heavy recreational boat and paddle traffic in the afternoon.
